name: Single-pane wood frame (historic baseline)
description: Pre-1970s historic; common in older MA housing stock
note: Baseline only — never code compliant. Auditor reference for vintage homes.

name: Double-pane vinyl, clear air-fill
note: Below current code U-factor. Common 1990s replacement.

name: Double-pane vinyl, soft Low-E, air-fill
note: IECC2021 R402.1.2 CZ5 max U=0.30 — borderline; verify NFRC label.

name: Double-pane vinyl, soft Low-E, argon-fill
note: Most-common MA new-construction window. Meets IECC and MA Stretch Code.

name: Double-pane fiberglass, soft Low-E, argon
note: Lower air leakage and U-factor; meets MA Specialized fenestration max U=0.25.

name: Double-pane wood-clad, soft Low-E, argon
note: Wood-aluminum clad; depending on the specific NFRC product, can meet specialized.

name: Double-pane aluminum (thermal break), Low-E, argon
note: Aluminum thermal-break frames typically do not meet IECC residential CZ5 U≤0.30. Avoid in MA new construction.

name: Triple-pane vinyl, two soft Low-E surfaces, argon
note: Common Passive House baseline; meets MA Specialized.

name: Triple-pane fiberglass, two Low-E, argon
note: Passive-House class window; very low air leakage. Premium price ($60–120/sq ft).

name: Triple-pane wood, two Low-E, krypton fill
note: Krypton cuts U below argon; ~30% premium over argon. Specify only where target is U-0.15 or lower.

name: Triple-pane vinyl, spectrally-selective high-SHGC south-facing
note: High SHGC for south-facing passive-solar; specified per facade in PHIUS designs.

name: Vinyl skylight, double-pane Low-E argon
note: Skylight U-factor max IECC R402.1.2 CZ5 U=0.55. Most domes/curbs run higher than vertical windows.

name: Fiberglass sliding patio door, Low-E argon
note: Slider doors fall in fenestration; meet U-0.30 for CZ5 base.

name: Storm window added over double-pane vinyl, total assembly
note: Adding a storm window to existing double-pane improves U by ~30%. Common WAP measure.
